subject: Public-Key-Pins (HPKP) header
truth: Do not use Public-Key-Pins; remove any HPKP headers from production.
quote: Remove any `Public-Key-Pins` or `Public-Key-Pins-Report-Only` headers from production.
source: OWASP CS: HTTP Security Response Headers Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Laravel debug mode in production
truth: Set APP_DEBUG=false in production to prevent information disclosure.
quote: Make sure your app is not in debug mode while in production
source: OWASP CS: Laravel Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RSA key size
truth: Use RSA keys of at least 2048 bits; do not use weaker key sizes.
quote: select a key size of at least 2048 bits when using RSA keys
source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: TLS for all pages
truth: Use TLS for all pages, not just sensitive ones like login pages.
quote: TLS should be used for all pages, not just those that are considered sensitive such as the login page.
source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: OpenID 2.0 is obsolete
truth: Do not implement OpenID 2.0; use OpenID Connect (OIDC) instead.
quote: > **Avoid confusion:** **OpenID 2.0 ("OpenID")** was a separate, legacy authentication protocol that has been **superseded by OpenID Connect** and is considered obsolete.
source: OWASP CS: Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Expect-CT header
truth: Do not use Expect-CT; remove it from existing code if possible.
quote: Mozilla [recommends](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Expect-CT) avoiding it, and removing it from existing code if possible.
source: OWASP CS: HTTP Security Response Headers Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: debugging enabled in production mobile app
truth: Disable debugging in production builds of mobile applications.
quote: Disable debugging.
source: OWASP CS: Mobile Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MFA not required for admin users
truth: Require MFA for administrative or other high-privileged users.
quote: Require MFA for administrative or other high privileged users
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: certificate hashing algorithm
truth: Use SHA-256 for certificate hashing; do not use MD5 or SHA-1.
quote: Certificates should use SHA-256 for the hashing algorithm, rather than the older MD5 and SHA-1 algorithms
source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: VPN replacement
truth: Replace VPNs with Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) instead.
quote: Use Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) instead
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging stopped without detection
truth: Enable processes to detect whether logging has stopped.
quote: Enable processes to detect whether logging has stopped
source: OWASP CS: Logging Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: hardcoded secrets in Django/DRF
truth: Stop hardcoding secrets; use a Secret Manager instead.
quote: Secrets should never be hardcoded. The best practice is to use a Secret Manager.
source: OWASP CS: Django REST Framework (DRF) Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: pinning in non-native mobile applications
truth: If it is not a native mobile application, do not pin.
quote: If it is not a native mobile application, do not pin.
source: OWASP CS: Pinning Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: transmitting secrets over plaintext
truth: Never transmit secrets via plaintext; always use TLS.
quote: Never transmit secrets via plaintext
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Hardcoded credentials in mobile app
truth: Do not hardcode credentials in the mobile app.
quote: Do not hardcode credentials in the mobile app.
source: OWASP CS: Mobile Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Django DEBUG mode in production
truth: Never run DEBUG = True in production.
quote: - Ensure that the application is never in `DEBUG` mode in a production environment.
source: OWASP CS: Django Security Cheat Sheet

subject: Sensitive data via SMS
truth: Avoid sending sensitive data via SMS.
quote: Avoid sending sensitive data via SMS.
